title: mCytoMAP-core: reference implementation of the Evidence-Tier prior layer
description: Reference implementation of the literature-derived Evidence-Tier prior layer of mCytoMAP, the cytoskeletal-checkpoint prioritisation framework of the Targetome Perspective Target-S2026-0032. A single dependency-free command (python3 reproduce_values.py) regenerates and asserts every per-node value reported in the manuscript: the Evidence-Tier Scores (Tables 1A/1B), the two-tier prior pi_norm (Figure 3C), the validated/candidate tier sums (1.00 / 0.21), and the per-cell ETS(node | sex, context) values (Supplementary Table S1). The package is deterministic (no randomness, network, clock dependence, or external data), runs on the Python ≥ 3.10 standard library alone, ships committed machine-readable outputs with SHA-256 provenance, an assertion test-suite run in CI on Python 3.10-3.12, and structured documentation (architecture, reproducibility, validation, provenance), a pinned Docker container for environment-independent reproduction, and a committed exact console transcript checked byte-for-byte in CI. It implements exactly, and only, the prior layer whose values appear in the manuscript.
